sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after MOUSE(row=3, col=20)
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation

prev_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes']
curr_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in curr_seg['nodes']

print("Previous nodes count:", len(prev_nodes))
print("Current nodes count:", len(curr_nodes)

# Check for any differences
for nid in curr_nodes:
    if nid in prev_nodes:
        p = prev_nodes[nid]
        c = curr_nodes[nid]
        if p['color'] != c['color'] or p['pixels'] != c['pixels'] or p['hash'] != c['hash']:
            print(f"Node {nid} changed: color {p['color']}->{c['color']}, pixels {p['pixels']}->{c['pixels']}

# Check new/disappeared nodes
for nid in curr_nodes:
    if nid not in prev_nodes:
        print("New node:", nid, curr_nodes[nid]['color'], curr_nodes[nid]['pixels']
for nid in prev_nodes:
    if nid not in curr_nodes:
        print("Disappeared node:", nid, prev_nodes[nid]['color'], prev_nodes[nid]['pixels']

# Also check last_action_result
print("Result:", last_action_result)
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"world_model": "Level 1. Top: 4 colored blocks (b, N, Y, p) - 20px each at row 3. Middle: red frame with 4 gray dots. Bottom: 4 colored blocks (N, p, b, Y) - 16px each.
goal_model: Match top colors with bottom blocks.
action_model: MOUSE to select/move blocks.
recent_findings: Clicked on blue top block (b) at (3,20). Need to see what changed.
open_questions: How do actions work? What does MOUSE do? Did anything change?
plan: Inspect diff between prev and current
plan: Check diff, then test more probing.
cross_level_notes: None.
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
